Documentation","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-11-20T15:35:36Z","updated_at":"2024-11-20T16:40:09Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Issue with current documentation:

The docs [here](https://numpy.org/doc/2.1/reference/random/generated/numpy.random.Generator.choice.html) say that:

> If an int, the random sample is generated from np.arange(a).

I expected an `np.int64` but it returns an `int`. `np.arange(a)` by default returns an array of dtype `np.int64`, so the sample is not directly from there. 

Further, I also expected an np.int64 to be returned when I run:
`np.random.default_rng(100).choice(np.int64(5))`, however I get `int`. 

In principle, I feel this could also be a _normal_ bug, but given that `np.random.default_rng(100).integers(5)` returns an `np.int64` with either type of scalar input and adding `size=()`, as in `np.random.default_rng(100).choice(np.int64(5),size=())` can be used as a workaround to get an `np.int64` as the return type when using `choice()`, I am guessing this behaviour will not be changed but I feel that this should at least be documented because it led to confusion on my end when I was debugging something. 

For reference, here are different ablations I ran to understand the current behaviour:
```
type(np.random.default_rng(100).choice(np.int64(5)))
<class 'int'>
type(np.random.default_rng(100).choice(np.int64(5),1))
<class 'numpy.ndarray'>
np.random.default_rng(100).choice(np.int64(5),1).dtype
dtype('int64')
np.random.default_rng(100).integers(5).dtype
dtype('int64')
```

Additionally, the "Returns" part of the docs says:
> samples : single item or ndarray

I believe the "single item" should be replaced with "Python int". 

I could open a pull request to change the docs or file this as a bug if you think the behaviour should be changed. Please let me know.

Indeed, sorry about the oversight.

> Actually creating that intermediate would be wasteful and unnecessary.

I see. I'm curious now about why `choice()` and `integers()` differ in their default return types for the case when I pass in an integer to either of them. For me the default of `int` only in the case of a single integer being with `choice()` passed turned out to be magnified into a bigger issue because of type checking going on in some other library code deep down in the call stack leading to unexpected behaviour on the outside (both `choice()` and `integers()` return `np.int64` by default in the other cases).

Documentation","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-11-20T15:35:36Z","updated_at":"2024-11-21T11:47:12Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Issue with current documentation:

The docs [here](https://numpy.org/doc/2.1/reference/random/generated/numpy.random.Generator.choice.html) say that:

> If an int, the random sample is generated from np.arange(a).

I expected an `np.int64` but it returns an `int`. `np.arange(a)` by default returns an array of dtype `np.int64`, so the sample is not directly from there. 

Further, I also expected an np.int64 to be returned when I run:
`np.random.default_rng(100).choice(np.int64(5))`, however I get `int`. 

In principle, I feel this could also be a _normal_ bug, but given that `np.random.default_rng(100).integers(5)` returns an `np.int64` with either type of scalar input and adding `size=()`, as in `np.random.default_rng(100).choice(np.int64(5),size=())` can be used as a workaround to get an `np.int64` as the return type when using `choice()`, I am guessing this behaviour will not be changed but I feel that this should at least be documented because it led to confusion on my end when I was debugging something. 

For reference, here are different ablations I ran to understand the current behaviour:
```
type(np.random.default_rng(100).choice(np.int64(5)))
<class 'int'>
type(np.random.default_rng(100).choice(np.int64(5),1))
<class 'numpy.ndarray'>
np.random.default_rng(100).choice(np.int64(5),1).dtype
dtype('int64')
np.random.default_rng(100).integers(5).dtype
dtype('int64')
```

Additionally, the "Returns" part of the docs says:
> samples : single item or ndarray

I believe the "single item" should be replaced with "Python int". 

I could open a pull request to change the docs or file this as a bug if you think the behaviour should be changed. Please let me know.
